---
title: ""
---

::: {.home-shell}
::: {.profile-sidebar}
![](images/profile/markuss-headshot.jpg){.profile-photo}

<div class="profile-focus">Data Science</div>

### Markuss Saule

<div class="identity-line">Decision-grade analytics for healthcare, markets, and operations</div>

<div class="profile-disciplines"><span class="discipline-pill">Business Analytics</span><span class="discipline-pill">Statistics</span></div>

I am a systems-first analytics builder focused on operational risk, bottlenecks, and decision thresholds in complex organizations.

<div class="profile-links">
<a class="btn-primary" href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/markuss-saule/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">LinkedIn</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="files/resume.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Resume PDF</a>
</div>

<div class="profile-meta">
<div><span>GPA</span><strong>4.0</strong></div>
<div><span>Degree</span><strong>BS Business Analytics</strong></div>
<div><span>Minors</span><strong>Statistics, Data Science</strong></div>
<div><span>Core Stack</span><strong>Python, SQL, R, Power BI</strong></div>
</div>
:::

::: {.home-main}
::: {.hero-panel}
<div class="kicker">Decision-Grade Analytics for Complex Systems</div>

# I build decision engines, not decorative dashboards.

I am a Business Analytics student at BYU-Idaho building high-consequence analytics for healthcare, market systems, and enterprise operations. My work is designed for environments where tradeoffs matter and decisions carry real cost.

Healthcare is personal to me. Family health challenges shaped how I define impact, and why I care about platform-level systems that improve outcomes at scale.

<div class="hero-actions">
<a class="btn-primary" href="projects.html">View Projects</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="resume.html">Experience Timeline</a>
</div>
:::

## New Flagship

<div class="section-intro">MERCURY extends the portfolio beyond dashboards and into full-system market design: exchange mechanics, stress testing, fragmented venues, and reproducible research artifacts.</div>

::: {.flagship-panel}
<img class="flagship-banner" src="images/projects/mercury-market-sim/cover.png" alt="MERCURY benchmark landscape">
<div class="flagship-copy">
<div class="eyebrow">New flagship build</div>

### MERCURY: Market Microstructure Simulation Lab

MERCURY is a research-grade multi-agent exchange simulator built around a continuous double auction, endogenous price formation, heterogeneous trading agents, fragmented venues, and fee-aware venue economics.

It combines the engine and the communication layer: benchmark suites, parameter sweeps, publication-grade plots, and a reproducible research report that makes the system legible without opening the codebase first.

<div class="flagship-stats">
<div class="flagship-stat"><strong>18</strong><span>built-in benchmark scenarios</span></div>
<div class="flagship-stat"><strong>67</strong><span>passing tests across the simulation core</span></div>
<div class="flagship-stat"><strong>45 to 9</strong><span>flash-crash detections after circuit breaker intervention</span></div>
<div class="flagship-stat"><strong>0.8201</strong><span>mean cross-asset dislocation captured in spillover runs</span></div>
</div>

<div class="hero-actions">
<a class="btn-primary" href="projects/mercury-market-sim.html">Explore MERCURY</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="files/mercury-market-sim/research_report.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Open Research Report</a>
</div>
</div>
:::

## Operating Doctrine

::: {.operating-grid}
<div class="op-card"><h4>Systems First</h4><p>I model the full system before choosing metrics. Local optimization without systems context is noise.</p></div>
<div class="op-card"><h4>Risk Explicitly</h4><p>I surface thresholds, failure modes, and confidence bounds so leaders can act under uncertainty.</p></div>
<div class="op-card"><h4>Bottlenecks Before Features</h4><p>I prioritize constraints that limit throughput, access, and quality before adding complexity.</p></div>
<div class="op-card"><h4>Tradeoffs Over Vanity KPIs</h4><p>I design analytics around decision consequences, not dashboard aesthetics.</p></div>
:::

## Selected Outcomes

<div class="metric-strip">
<div class="metric-pill"><strong>1,150+</strong><span>Qualified applicants from optimized Meta campaigns</span></div>
<div class="metric-pill"><strong>320%</strong><span>Hiring engagement lift from KPI dashboarding</span></div>
<div class="metric-pill"><strong>70%</strong><span>Reporting time reduction via automation</span></div>
<div class="metric-pill"><strong>114K+</strong><span>SEER patient records modeled for survival analysis</span></div>
</div>

## Featured Projects

::: {.project-grid}
<a class="project-card" href="projects/mercury-market-sim.html"><img class="project-thumb" src="images/projects/mercury-market-sim/cover.png" alt="MERCURY benchmark landscape"><div class="project-body"><div class="card-topline"><span class="project-badge">Flagship</span></div><div class="card-title">MERCURY: Market Microstructure Simulation Lab</div><p>Research-grade exchange simulation for liquidity stress, flash crashes, venue fragmentation, and fee-aware strategy interaction.</p><div class="chips"><span class="chip">Python</span><span class="chip">Simulation</span><span class="chip">Market Microstructure</span></div></div></a>
<a class="project-card" href="projects/fulfillment.html"><img class="project-thumb" src="images/projects/fulfillment/cover.png" alt="Fulfillment simulation visual"><div class="project-body"><div class="card-title">Fulfillment Center Risk Simulation and Staffing Optimization</div><p>SimPy-based engine with pressure scoring, risk signals, and staffing search for high-load operations.</p><div class="chips"><span class="chip">Python</span><span class="chip">SimPy</span><span class="chip">Optimization</span></div></div></a>
<a class="project-card" href="projects/blockchain-fraud.html"><img class="project-thumb" src="images/projects/blockchain-fraud/cover.png" alt="Blockchain fraud architecture"><div class="project-body"><div class="card-title">Blockchain + ML Healthcare Fraud Ledger</div><p>XGBoost fraud scoring combined with tamper-evident records for trust and auditability.</p><div class="chips"><span class="chip">XGBoost</span><span class="chip">SHA-256</span><span class="chip">Streamlit</span></div></div></a>
<a class="project-card" href="projects/insurance-fraud.html"><img class="project-thumb" src="images/projects/insurance-fraud/cover.png" alt="Insurance model performance chart"><div class="project-body"><div class="card-title">Insurance Fraud Detection</div><p>Model comparison from logistic regression to XGBoost with SHAP explainability and deployment artifacts.</p><div class="chips"><span class="chip">scikit-learn</span><span class="chip">XGBoost</span><span class="chip">SHAP</span></div></div></a>
<a class="project-card" href="projects/ai-scheduling.html"><img class="project-thumb" src="images/projects/ai-scheduling/cover.png" alt="Scheduling dashboard"><div class="project-body"><div class="card-title">AI-Powered Scheduling and Patient Access Optimization</div><p>Healthcare operations dashboard with forecasting, decomposition, and capacity visibility.</p><div class="chips"><span class="chip">Power BI</span><span class="chip">Forecasting</span><span class="chip">Healthcare Ops</span></div></div></a>
<a class="project-card" href="projects/readmission.html"><img class="project-thumb" src="images/projects/readmission/cover.png" alt="Readmission database visual"><div class="project-body"><div class="card-title">Hospital Readmission Analytics</div><p>SQL schema design + R modeling + BI outputs to isolate readmission risk drivers and equity patterns.</p><div class="chips"><span class="chip">SQL</span><span class="chip">R</span><span class="chip">Power BI</span></div></div></a>
:::

<div class="center-link"><a class="btn-ghost" href="projects.html">See full project archive</a></div>
:::
:::
